assertions: First town door visit opened the bank.
Title to town door to keep entry succeeded without reveal.
First depth-1 objective resolved before any reveal hook.
Town return fell through to the bank-first directive and still exposed the bank plan.
Unified telemetry retained the canonical first-run id with no duplicate search stream.
Depth-1 placement stress passed 60 starts without no-path or touching-room failures.
Unified telemetry store retained recent events after stress coverage and captured the death probe.

snapshots.label: title-screen
snapshots.title: Castle of the Winds
snapshots.copy: Descend the keep. Claim the Runestone. Return alive.

snapshots.label: town-start
snapshots.location: The valley town below the keep

directive.primaryText: Open one town door to see what town can do.
directive.supportText: Then follow the north road straight into the keep.

feedLines: Town Door: Open one town door to see what town can do.
Town is quiet.
Open any labeled town door once. Then return to the north road.

feedback:
Strengths
- The board-first HUD reads quickly in motion. Health, threat, and objective framing stay visible without burying the board.
- Town service screens are easy to parse once opened. The bank/meta layer is especially clear about what gold can do beyond shopping.
- The floor objective gate gives the first dungeon level a stronger purpose than a simple stair rush.
- Objective automation covered 4/4 current depth-1 objective types in repeat runs.
- Meta review: 100% of tracked runs started with a contract, and bank persistence was revisited after 0/0 successful returns.
- First-objective pacing averages: clear turn 48, searches before clear 0.5.

Friction
- The first-time flow is improved, but it still leans on automation to brute-force objective variants and room states. The title and town guidance are now legible enough that the harness can follow the keep route directly without a full-floor reveal.
- The live feed is now visible in the main HUD, but it still needs repeated phone checks to confirm the extra lines never crowd the board.
- The first search revealed 17 additional route points without fully revealing the floor.
- Placement stress: 0 no-path cases and 0 touching-room cases across 60 depth-1 starts.
- The first objective (Purge The Nest) resolved without needing a full map reveal. Coverage for the other depth-1 variants now uses the same no-reveal objective pass.

Run Notes
- Objective on depth 1: Purge The Nest
- Final state: The valley town below the keep, turn 177, HP 56/56
- Search probe ran before objective completion to verify route extension without full-floor reveal.
- Review scenarios captured: first-descent, first-visible-threat, spell-blast-preview, extract-or-greed, town-return-bank.
